one_line_takeaway: ARDEL layers a per-input dynamically-weighted BERT/RoBERTa/ALBERT ensemble on top of RSMI and adversarial training, reporting large accuracy-under-attack gains on AG News/IMDB, but its evaluation reuses the training attacks, lacks an adaptive attack and cost analysis, and only partially ablates its own components.
key_finding: ARDEL reports large, consistent gains in accuracy-under-attack over all four baselines across the three attacks and three PLMs. On AG News with BERT-Base, accuracy under TextFooler rises from 10.20% (fine-tuned) and 52.60% (RSMI, the strongest baseline) to 82.70% for ARDEL, while clean accuracy also improves to 96.80%. On IMDB with BERT-Base, under-attack accuracy under TextFooler goes from 7.70% (fine-tuned) to 84.80% (ARDEL). Attacker query counts also rise (e.g., AG News BERT under TextBugger: 372 fine-tuned to 1120 ARDEL; IMDB BERT under TextBugger: 534 to 4380), indicating attacks are harder to execute. The QNLI/MNLI ablation isolates only 'RSMI + Ensemble' (not the full ARDEL meta-model/detector) and shows it lifts QNLI TextFooler accuracy from 35.2% (RSMI alone) to 61.4%.
core_contribution: The paper proposes ARDEL (Adversarial Robustness through Dynamic Ensemble Learning), a defense scheme for pre-trained language models (PLMs) that combines a diverse ensemble of BERT, RoBERTa, and ALBERT with (i) a meta-model that assigns per-input dynamic weights to each base model, (ii) an adversarial-pattern-detection module, and (iii) Randomized Smoothing + Masked Inference (RSMI) plus adversarial training and dropout regularization. Its distinguishing claim versus prior static ensembles is that the ensemble composition/weighting is reconfigured per input based on detected adversarial patterns, down-weighting models judged susceptible to the detected attack and up-weighting more robust ones. Note: DiffuseDef, FreeLB, defensive distillation, InfoBERT, and RSMI itself are cited RELATED WORK / baselines, not ARDEL's own contribution; ARDEL reuses RSMI (Moon et al.) as an internal component.
threat_model: Adversary crafts adversarial text examples to flip a PLM classifier's prediction at inference time, across three granularities evaluated: word-level synonym substitution (TextFooler), sub-word substitution (BERT-Attack), and character-level perturbation (TextBugger). Attacks are query-based via the TextAttack library under BERT-Attack evaluation constraints. The defense goal is to raise accuracy-under-attack and the number of queries an attacker needs for a successful evasion. The paper does not construct an adaptive/white-box attack against ARDEL's own defense components, and provides no certified robustness guarantee.
limitations: 1) The NLI ablation evaluates 'RSMI + Ensemble' on BERT only and never runs full ARDEL there, so the individual contributions of the meta-model and the OneClassSVM pattern detector are not isolated on any dataset. 2) The exact same attack recipes used to GENERATE adversarial training data (TextFooler, TextBugger, BERT-Attack) are the ones used at evaluation, so the reported robustness may partly reflect train/test attack overlap rather than generalization to unseen attacks. 3) No adaptive attack is mounted against ARDEL's stochastic components (randomized smoothing, masking); such defenses are historically vulnerable to gradient-masking-aware attacks and the paper offers no certified guarantee. 4) No computational cost, latency, or memory figures are reported despite running three PLMs plus a meta-model plus a detector per input. 5) Several ARDEL clean-accuracy numbers are essentially flat vs fine-tuned baselines (e.g., IMDB BERT 93.80% vs 93.30%), and the uniformly ~80%+ under-attack accuracy across every setting is unusually high relative to the RSMI baseline it builds on, warranting independent replication. 6) The paper is a short workshop-style scheme description; some implementation details (meta-model input features, detector integration into weighting) are described only at a high level.

quantitative_results: AG News, BERT-Base, accuracy under TextFooler: Fine-Tuned 10.20%, RSMI 52.60%, ARDEL 82.70%
AG News, BERT-Base, clean accuracy (OA): Fine-Tuned 94.40%, ARDEL 96.80%
AG News, RoBERTa-Base, accuracy under TextBugger: Fine-Tuned 36.90%, RSMI 66.40%, ARDEL 86.40%
IMDB, BERT-Base, accuracy under TextFooler: Fine-Tuned 7.70%, RSMI 60.00%, ARDEL 84.80%
IMDB, RoBERTa-Base, accuracy under BERT-Attack: Fine-Tuned 13.60%, RSMI 70.60%, ARDEL 86.10%
AG News, BERT-Base, attacker queries under TextBugger: Fine-Tuned 372, RSMI 737, ARDEL 1120
IMDB, BERT-Base, attacker queries under TextBugger: Fine-Tuned 534, RSMI 3455, ARDEL 4380
QNLI ablation, BERT-Base, accuracy under TextFooler: Fine-Tuned 21.5%, RSMI 35.2%, RSMI+Ensemble 61.4%
MNLI ablation, BERT-Base, accuracy under TextFooler: Fine-Tuned 25.3%, RSMI 31.1%, RSMI+Ensemble 60.8%
Evaluation protocol: clean accuracy on full test set; under-attack accuracy and query counts on random 1000-example subset; averaged over 5 random seeds
baselines_compared: RSMI (randomized smoothing + masked inference, Moon et al.)
